recalls.Description: Specialized is recalling all Turbo Como SL electric bicycles due to a potential safety issue with the bicycle forks. The model name "Como SL" can be found on the seat tube or top tube, and these bikes were available in various colors such as Cast Umber, Dove Grey, Raspberry (Como SL 4.0), and Smoke, Brassy Yellow, and Brushed Silver (Como SL 5.0). To identify your specific model, you can connect your bicycle to the Specialized app. Only the SL versions within the Como family are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Specialized Recalls Turbo Como SL Electric Bicycles Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Specialized Rider Care at 877-808-8154 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email ridercare@specialized.com, or visit www.specialized.com for more information, including safety recall notices.

Products.Name: Specialized Turbo Como SL 4.0 Bicycle Fork

Images.Caption: Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 4.0 in Raspberry
Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 4.0 in Cast Umber
Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 4.0 in Dove Grey
Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 5.0 in Brassy Yellow
Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 5.0 in Brushed Silver
Como SL e-bike with recalled fork steerer tube - Model 5.0 in Smoke

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an incident involving a steerer tube failing and breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.specialized.com and through Authorized Specialized Retailers from March 2021 to January 2026, priced between $3,250 and $4,800.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential fatigue crack in the fork steerer tube

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using their Turbo Como SL bicycles immediately and reach out to their Authorized Specialized Retailer for a complimentary fork replacement. Replacement parts are anticipated to be available in Spring 2026.

recalls.metaDescription: Specialized recalls Turbo Como SL electric bicycles due to a fall hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Gazelle USA has announced a recall of the Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes equipped with Fidlock buckles. These bicycles are available in a single size and two color options: green and black. Constructed with an aluminum frame and a reinforced foam (EPP) cargo box, each bike measures 105 inches in length and 28 inches in width, with 'Urban Arrow' printed on the underside of the cargo box.
recalls.Title: Gazelle USA Recalls Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Gazelle USA at 800-838-5388, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email service.na@urbanarrow.com or visit https://urbanarrow.com/en-na/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes

Images.Caption: Recalled FamilyNext Pro Cargo Bike
Recalled E-bike Old Buckle (left) and E-bike Replacement Buckle (right)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of buckles not fully latching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at select specialty stores from March 2025 to December 2025 for approximately $9,000.

Importers.Name: Gazelle USA, LLC, of Santa Cruz, California

Hazards.Name: Buckle may not fully latch, creating a risk of injury to children in the cargo area.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the e-bikes immediately and contact Gazelle USA for a complimentary repair of the cargo area bench assembly, which includes a buckle replacement at a dealership.

recalls.metaDescription: Gazelle USA recalls Urban Arrow FamilyNext Pro Cargo E-Bikes due to a buckle failure risk, urging consumers to seek repairs immediately.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the rear wheel bolts on 2026 model Trek FX+ 1 Midstep, FX+ 1S Midstep, FX+ 1 Stepover, and FX+ 1S Stepover electric bicycles, as well as Electra Townie Go! Step Thru and Townie Go! S Step Thru models sold prior to October 28, 2025. These bicycles are available in multiple colors and sizes, with distinct branding on the downtube and other parts. The affected bolts are black and connect the motor to the rear frame, posing a risk of breaking and causing the wheel to detach, which could lead to falls.
recalls.Title: Recall of Trek and Electra Electric Bicycle Rear Wheel Bolts Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ for more details.

Products.Name: Rear wheel bolts for 2026 Trek FX+ 1 electric bicycles

Images.Caption: Trek FX+1 Midstep with recalled rear wheel bolts
Trek FX+1S Midstep with recalled rear wheel bolts
Trek FX+ 1 Stepover with recalled rear wheel bolts
Trek FX+ 1S Stepover with recalled rear wheel bolts
Electra Townie Go! Step Thru with recalled rear wheel bolts
Electra Townie Go! S Step Thru with recalled rear wheel bolts

Injuries.Name: The firm has received seven reports of the rear wheel bolts breaking on affected bicycles. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Trek stores and authorized retailers across the nation, as well as online, from April 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $2,100.

Hazards.Name: Risk of wheel separation due to breaking bolts

Remedies.Name: Free replacement of black bolts with new silver bolts

recalls.metaDescription: Trek and Electra recall electric bicycle rear wheel bolts due to fall hazards. Contact Trek for free repairs and store credit.

recalls.Description: PNW Components is recalling the Loam Pedal Gen 2, an aluminum alloy mountain bike pedal, due to a potential safety issue. These platform-style pedals, identifiable by the 'Tectonic' logo etched on the left corner, may crack at the axle, leading to detachment and posing a fall hazard for riders. The pedals are available in various colors, including black, silver, green, orange, and purple.
recalls.Title: PNW Components Recalls Loam Pedal Gen 2 Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact PNW Components, Inc. at 833-902-3381 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email info@pnwcomponents.com. More information can be found at https://www.pnwcomponents.com/pages/voluntary-recall-loam-pedal-gen-2 or by visiting www.pnwcomponents.com and clicking the Recall link in the footer.

Products.Name: Loam Pedal Gen 2 Aluminum Alloy Pedals

Images.Caption: Recalled Loam Pedal Gen 2 aluminum alloy bicycle pedal
Recalled Loam Pedals Gen 2 aluminum alloy bicycle pedals

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the pedal axle cracking and detaching from the crank. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through independent bicycle dealers and online at PNWComponents.com, REI.com, Backcountry.com, and JensonUSA.com from June 2025 to November 2025, priced around $160 per pair.

Importers.Name: PNW Components, Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of pedal detachment due to axle cracking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the pedals immediately and contact PNW Components for free replacement axles when available. In the meantime, PNW Components is offering the Range Pedal at no cost. Replacement axles are expected to ship starting January 5, 2026.

recalls.metaDescription: PNW Components recalls Loam Pedal Gen 2 due to a fall hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Pedego Electric Bikes is recalling its Fat Tire Trike, a three-wheeled electric tricycle made from aluminum. The trike features a rectangular label on the downtube with the name "Pedego" and was sold in a matte black finish. There is a risk that the frame may develop a hairline fracture near a weld, leading to potential falls and laceration injuries.
recalls.Title: Pedego Electric Bikes Recalls Fat Tire Trikes for Safety Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pedego at 800-646-8604 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at recall@pedego.com. More information is available online at https://pedegoelectricbikes.com/pages/product-recalls or by visiting www.pedegoelectricbikes.com and clicking on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pedego Fat Tire Trike

Injuries.Name: Pedego has received 16 reports of the trike frame breaking, including one report of a laceration to the leg.

Retailers.Name: Available at Pedego stores nationwide and online at PedegoElectricBikes.com from March 2024 through March 2025, priced between $3,500 and $4,000.

Importers.Name: Pedego, LLC, of Fountain View, California

Hazards.Name: Fall and laceration hazards due to potential frame fractures

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled Fat Tire Trikes and contact Pedego for a free frame repair, which will be performed by a Pedego dealer of their choice. A registration form can be completed at https://pedegoelectricbikes.com/pages/product-recalls.

recalls.metaDescription: Pedego recalls Fat Tire Trikes due to fall and laceration risks. Contact Pedego for repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: Pacific Cycle has announced a recall for the Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356. This carrier is designed in black with a gray seat cushion, featuring black harness straps and red buckles. It is attached to a rear bike rack that was included with the purchase. The affected date codes are 712A0329BE and 712A0421BE, which can be found on the underside of the carrier.
recalls.Title: Pacific Cycle Issues Recall for Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carriers Due to Potential Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled carriers and reach out to Pacific Cycle at 877-564-2261 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, via email at customerservice@pacific-cycle.com, or visit www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356
Recalled Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356 attached to rear bike rack (sold with carrier)

Injuries.Name: Pacific Cycle is aware of one incident where the buckles released. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and schwinnbikes.com from May 2025 to September 2025, priced between $190 and $230

Importers.Name: Pacific Cycle Inc., of Madison, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to buckle failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the carrier and request a full refund from Pacific Cycle

recalls.metaDescription: Pacific Cycle recalls Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carriers due to a fall hazard. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: TREK is recalling several electric bicycle models from the 2026 lineup, including the Domane+ ALR 5, Domane+ ALR 6 AXS, Checkpoint+ SL 6, and Checkpoint+ SL 7. These bikes are available in various colors and sizes, with the brand name 'TREK' displayed on the downtube and the model names on the top tube. The recall is due to a potential safety hazard where loose bolts on the chainring may cause it to detach, posing a risk of falls or crashes.
recalls.Title: TREK Recalls Electric Bicycles for Fall and Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Trek toll-free at 800-373-4594, available from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information can also be found online at https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/recalls or by visiting https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/ and clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled MY26 Domane+ ALR 5 Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Domane+ ALR 6 AXS Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Checkpoint+ SL 6 Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Checkpoint+ SL7 Electric Bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the bicycle chainring coming loose on the recalled bikes. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers and authorized Trek retailers across the country and online from July 2025 to September 2025, priced between $5,000 and $8,000.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Loose chainring bolts may lead to separation, causing fall or crash risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected bicycles and contact an authorized Trek dealer for a complimentary repair. Dealers will tighten the chainring bolts to the proper specifications. Additionally, participants in the recall will receive a $20 in-store credit for Trek, Electra, or Bontrager products, valid until December 31, 2026.

recalls.metaDescription: TREK recalls select 2026 electric bicycles due to potential fall hazards. Contact Trek for free repairs and store credit.

recalls.Description: Trek is recalling specific models of Trek and Electra bicycles equipped with coaster brakes, as well as replacement rear wheels. These bicycles come in various colors, including red, flamingo pink, dark aquatic, Mulsanne blue, volt yellow, pink frosting, Miami green, lithium gray, viper red, purple abyss, buttercup, pixie blue, and blue. To check if your bicycle or rear wheel is affected, please enter your serial number at https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/coasterbrake-recall-inquiry/. The serial number can be found under the bottom bracket between the pedals and begins with
recalls.Title: Trek Recalls Coaster Brake Bicycles and Rear Wheels Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ or www.trekbikes.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Trek and Electra Coaster Brake Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 68,000 (In addition, about 6,820 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Precaliber 20 Bicycle in Miami Green (MY 2024)
Recalled Trek Precaliber 12 Bicycle with Coaster Brakes in Viper - Red
Recalled Precaliber 16 Bicycle with Coaster Brakes in Volt - Yellow
Close Up of Replacement Wheel for Recalled Bicycles
Replacement Wheel for Recalled Bicycles

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Authorized Trek and Electra retailers across the nation, available both in-store and online, including Trek.com, from August 2023 to September 2025. Prices ranged from $300 to $660 for bicycles and $66 to $77 for replacement wheels.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to loss of control and potential crashes

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bicycles and rear wheels immediately and contact an authorized Trek or Electra dealer for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Trek recalls certain bicycles and rear wheels due to crash hazards. Check your bike's serial number for safety.

recalls.Description: Knog is recalling its Blinder 900 and Blinder 1300 Front Bicycle Lights due to potential fire hazards linked to overheating lithium-ion batteries. The affected lights are identifiable by batch codes: 0124AA, 0423AA, 0723AA, 0923AA, 1023AA, 1123AA, and 11A23AA, which can be found on the underside of the units. These bicycle lights feature a black exterior and are branded with 'knog' and 'Blinder' on the handle. They were sold both individually and as part of a set, complete with a mounting system. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly to prevent fire risks; they should not be thrown away in regular trash or recycling bins.
recalls.Title: Knog Recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 Bicycle Lights Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Knog at recall@knog.com, visit http://www.knog.com/recall, or navigate to https://us.knog.com/ and select 'Blinder Recall' under the Support section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Knog Blinder 900 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 900 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 1300 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 1300 Bicycle Light

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the battery catching on fire or exploding. No injuries reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers nationwide and online through Amazon from January 2023 to August 2025, priced between $60 and $100.

Importers.Name: Knog Pty Ltd, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bicycle light immediately and register at http://www.knog.com/recall for a free replacement. Dispose of lithium-ion batteries according to local regulations and do not discard the light until confirmation from Knog is received.

recalls.metaDescription: Knog recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 bicycle lights due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: Super Wheels Inc. is recalling Vaast A/1 bicycles and framesets due to a potential fall hazard. This recall affects 35 bicycle models and 15 frameset models, available in Berry Red, Sea Blue, Matte Black, and Black Forest Green. Each bike features the 'VAAST' logo on both sides of the upper down tube, with the model name 'Model A/1' located on the left side rear seat. Serial numbers for the affected products range from WAWAK0001 to WAWCK0450 and VFA1M0011 to VFA1M0015, found beneath the bike frame on the bottom bracket.
recalls.Title: Super Wheels Recalls Vaast A/1 Bicycles and Frames Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Super Wheels Inc. at 855-430-6240 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@vaastbikes.com, or visit www.vaastbikes.com/vaast-model-a1-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Vaast A/1 Bicycles and Frames
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,860 (in addition, about 4 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Vaast A/1 bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has had 22 consumers report frame fractures and three consumers report frame tube separation, one of which resulted in a minor injury after falling from the bike.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from November 2024 through August 2025, priced between $25 and $50.

Importers.Name: Super Wheels Inc. f/k/a Allite Inc., of Miamisburg, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to potential hairline fractures in the bicycle frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the bicycles and frames immediately and reach out to Super Wheels Inc. for a free replacement. After registering their bike at www.vaastbikes.com/contact-us/, they will receive a replacement frame and reimbursement (up to $200) for a bike shop technician to rebuild their bicycle.

recalls.metaDescription: Super Wheels recalls Vaast A/1 bicycles and frames due to fall risks. Contact for free replacements and safety information.

recalls.Description: DT Swiss is recalling its Carbon Fiber Road Wheels due to a potential crash hazard stemming from a defect that may compromise the wheels' structural integrity. The affected models include ERC 35mm/45mm, CRC 35mm/45mm, HEC 35mm/45mm, and ARC 50mm/55mm/65mm. These wheels were sold both as aftermarket products and as original equipment on various premium road bicycle brands. The DT Swiss ID code, which is crucial for identification, can be found on a label on the outer rim of the wheel, indicating 'DT Swiss ID' followed by a 7-digit number. All wheels with a DT Swiss ID code of 2740000 or higher are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: DT Swiss Recalls Carbon Fiber Road Wheels Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DT Swiss toll-free at 800-000-1994 from 8:30 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email Recall@dtswiss.com. More information is available online at https://www.dtswiss.com/recall or by visiting www.dtswiss.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: DT Swiss Carbon Fiber Road Wheels
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,000 (In addition, about 150 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DT Swiss Carbon Fiber Road Wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the outermost carbon layer separating around the rim flange. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: DT Swiss, of Grand Junction, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available through independent bicycle dealers across the nation and as original equipment on various bicycle brands from September 2024 to July 2025, priced between $1,000 and $1,880.

Hazards.Name: Potential crash hazard due to structural integrity defect

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled wheels and contact DT Swiss for a replacement. Register the recalled wheels at www.dtswiss.com/recall.

recalls.metaDescription: DT Swiss recalls Carbon Fiber Road Wheels due to a crash hazard. Check if your wheels are affected and learn how to get a replacement.

recalls.Description: FENGQS is recalling its F7 Pro electric bikes, which were manufactured with batteries on April 21, 2024. These e-bikes, available in blue, feature the brand name "FENGQS" on the downtube and the model number "F7 Pro" on the chain stay near the rear wheel. The battery label indicates a capacity of 21.5Ah and the production date of 2024/4/21.
recalls.Title: FENGQS F7 Pro Electric Bikes Recalled Due to Fire Risks; Potential for Serious Injury or Death
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled e-bikes and reach out to FENGQS at 607-307-2127 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday. They can also email recall@fengqs.work or visit www.fengqs.com/pages/recalls for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled FENGQS F7 Pro E-bike
The battery's capacity (21.5Ah) and the production date (2024/4/21) are printed on a label on the front of the battery

Injuries.Name: FENGQS has received 13 reports of e-bikes igniting, two resulting in fires with property damage totaling $4,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Fengqisi Car Industry Co., Ltd., dba FENGQS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2024 to December 2024, priced between $700 and $1,200.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition from the lithium-ion battery, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled e-bikes and contact FENGQS for a full refund. They must mark their name, the word "recalled," and the current date next to the battery's production date with a permanent marker, then submit a photo of this information to recall@fengqs.work. Proper disposal of the battery is crucial; it should not be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with local hazardous waste centers for safe disposal options.

recalls.metaDescription: FENGQS recalls F7 Pro electric bikes due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for consumer safety. Contact for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: VIVI is recalling 36-volt lithium-ion rechargeable batteries that were sold with specific "VIVI" brand e-bikes. The affected e-bike models can be identified through sales order documentation or the bicycle frames, while the battery model information is printed directly on the batteries. The recall includes various model numbers, which are detailed in the official notice. Consumers are advised to check their e-bikes and batteries against this list to determine if they are impacted by the recall.
recalls.Title: VIVI E-Bikes Lithium-ion Batteries Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to VIVI at 800-375-6103 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or visit https://viviebikes.com/pages/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Affected VIVI e-bike model number C26
Affected VIVI e-bike model number MT20
Affected VIVI e-bike model number Z3
Affected VIVI e-bike model number M026SH
Affected VIVI e-bike model number H6
Affected VIVI e-bike model number H7
Affected VIVI e-bike model number MT26H
Affected VIVI e-bike model number 26LGB
Affected VIVI e-bike model number M026TGB
Affected VIVI e-bike model number MT26G
Affected VIVI e-bike model number FM20
Affected VIVI e-bike model number F20
Affected VIVI e-bike model number S3
Affected VIVI e-bike model number Z1
Affected VIVI e-bike model number Z2
Affected VIVI e-bike models label (located on the frame of e-bikes sold in 2022 and after)
Recalled VIVI e-bike 36-volt lithium-ion battery number label (located on the top or bottom of the battery casing depending on the battery model)

Injuries.Name: VIVI is aware of 14 reports of incidents of batteries overheating, including three reports of fires. No injuries reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Guangzhou Plenty Bicycle Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online through Amazon, Walmart, eBay, VIVI's website, Wish, Sears, Wayfair, and AliExpress from December 2020 to November 2023, priced between $365 and $950.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Binxin Household Co., Ltd., d/b/a VIVI E-bikes, of China

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating batteries

Remedies.Name: Free replacement battery and charger; proper disposal instructions

recalls.metaDescription: VIVI recalls lithium-ion batteries for e-bikes due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement and proper disposal instructions.

recalls.Description:
SPPTTY is recalling its kids bicycles due to hazardous lead levels that exceed federal safety standards. These bicycles, available in pink with brown accents, come in 14-inch and 18-inch sizes. The brand name 'SPPTTY' and 'New Sport Bike' are displayed on the downtube, making them easily identifiable. Parents should be aware of the potential health risks associated with lead exposure, particularly for young children.

The recall is prompted by the presence of lead in various components of the bicycles, including the black paint on the bicycle pumps. Lead can pose serious health risks if ingested, leading to potential poisoning. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure the safety of their children.
recalls.Title: SPPTTY Kids Bicycles Recalled Due to Lead Poisoning Risks; Sold Exclusively at Walmart
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology via email at lishkjie@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled SPPTTY Kids 14-inch Bicycle in Pink
Recalled SPPTTY Kids 18-inch Bicycle in Pink

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart.com from August 2024 through December 2024 for approximately $85.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology, dba SPPTTY, of China

Hazards.Name: Lead Poisoning Hazard due to Excessive Lead Levels

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles and contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology for a refund. Dismantle the bicycles, photograph them, and email the image to lishkjie@163.com. After verification, dispose of the bicycles.

recalls.metaDescription: SPPTTY recalls kids bicycles due to lead poisoning risks. Immediate action required for a refund. Contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology.

recalls.Description: Giant Bicycle is recalling the Momentum Vida E+ E-bikes due to a potential crash hazard. These bikes are available in several colors including metallic red, pearl white, metallic navy blue, metallic green, indigo blue, happy mint, and panther black. They feature two frame styles—mid-step and low-step—in small, medium, and large sizes. Each bike has a unique serial number located on the left rear dropout and a sticker under the downtube, with "Momentum" on the downtube and "Vida E+" on the top tube.
recalls.Title: Giant Bicycle Recalls Momentum Vida E+ E-Bikes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Giant Bicycle toll-free at 866-458-2555 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email at recall@giantbicycle.com, or visit https://www.momentum-biking.com/us/recall-information, https://www.giant-bicycles.com/us/recall-information, or https://www.liv-cycling.com/us/recall-info for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic green)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic red)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (pearl white)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic navy)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (indigo)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (panther)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (happy mint)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of broken fork steerer tubes. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Giant Bicycle dealers across the country and online at https://www.momentum.com/us from September 2019 through May 2025, priced between $2,500 and $3,200.

Importers.Name: Giant Bicycle Inc., of Newbury Park, California

Hazards.Name: Fork steerer tube may crack or break, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Bikes with serial numbers having the third character as 'H', 'J', or 'K' will receive a free fork replacement. Those with 'G' require a visual inspection by an authorized Giant Bicycle dealer.

recalls.metaDescription: Giant Bicycle recalls Momentum Vida E+ E-bikes due to a fall risk. Check serial numbers for safety compliance and contact for free repairs.

recalls.Description: Specialized Bicycle Components is recalling specific Globe-branded Haul ST and Haul LT e-bikes due to a safety concern with the telescopic seat posts. These 13-inch seat posts, which were included as standard equipment, may break when fully extended, creating a potential fall hazard for riders. The affected models are identifiable by the names 'Haul ST' and 'Haul LT' printed on the downtube of the bicycles.
recalls.Title: Recall of Specialized Globe Haul ST and LT E-Bikes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Specialized Bicycle Components at 877-808-8154, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, reach out via email at ridercare@specialized.com, use the Live Chat feature at http://www.specialized.com, or visit http://www.specialized.com/safety-recall-notices for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled HAUL-ST e-bike
Recalled Haul LT e-bike

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of incidents, including two minor injuries consisting of scrapes and bruises.

Retailers.Name: Authorized Specialized retailers and bicycle shops across the country, as well as online at www.specialized.com, from January 2023 to March 2025, priced between $2,500 and $3,500.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking seat post when fully extended, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the affected e-bikes and contact an authorized Specialized retailer for a complimentary repair, which involves replacing the seat post with one made from a different material.

recalls.metaDescription: Specialized recalls Globe Haul ST and LT e-bikes due to a fall hazard from faulty seat posts. Contact for free repairs.

recalls.Description: Santa Cruz Bicycles has announced a recall of its Heckler 9 electric bikes equipped with a 720W lithium-ion battery, which is housed in a black case under the bike frame. The bicycles feature the name "SANTA CRUZ" on the downtube and "Heckler" on the top tube and inside the left chainstay. Available in various colors such as Gloss Avocado Green and Maritime Gray, these bikes present a potential fire hazard due to the risk of the battery overheating. Proper disposal of the recalled battery is crucial, as it cannot be thrown away in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, consumers should contact their local household hazardous waste collection center for guidance on safe disposal methods.
recalls.Title: Santa Cruz Bicycles Issues Recall for Heckler 9 Electric Bikes Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Santa Cruz Bicycles toll-free at 833-944-8335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit their website at https://santacruzbicycles.com/pages/recall-heckler-9-lithium-ion-battery for more details.

Products.Name: Heckler 9 Electric Bikes
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,850 (In addition, about 550 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle
Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle serial number on battery pack

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of fires, including some property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Santa Cruz dealers across the U.S. from October 2021 to January 2025, priced between $8,750 and $13,900.

Distributors.Name: Santa Cruz Bicycles LLC, of Santa Cruz, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of battery overheating leading to fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bike, disconnect the battery, and check the manufacturer's website for registration and replacement battery details.

recalls.metaDescription: Santa Cruz recalls Heckler 9 electric bikes due to fire hazards from overheating batteries. Learn how to address this issue safely.

recalls.Description: Specialized Bicycle Components has issued a recall for its Vado and Como IGH e-bikes equipped with chain/belt guards. These e-bikes feature an internal gear hub and lack a rear derailleur. The brand name 'Specialized Bicycle' is displayed on the bike frame, and they are available in various colors including black, red, navy, and more. The model numbers for the recalled e-bikes can be found on the white and black serial number label located under the top tube of the bicycle.
recalls.Title: Recall of Specialized Vado and Como IGH E-Bikes Due to Potential Fall Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Specialized Bicycle Components at 877-808-8154 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, via email at ridercare@specialized.com, or visit http://www.specialized.com/safety-recall-notices for more details. Live Chat is also available at http://www.specialized.com.

Products.Name: Specialized Vado IGH E-Bike
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,400 (In addition, about 3,200 were sold in Canada and about 190 were sold in Mexico)

Recalled COMO SL 5.0 bicycle
Recalled COMO 5.0 IGH bicycle
Recalled COMO 4.0 IGH bicycle

Recalled VADO 5.0 IGH bicycle

Recalled Vado 3.0 IGH Step Through UL bicycle

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Authorized Specialized Bicycle retailers across the country and online, available from March 2021 through November 2024, priced between $3,250 and $5,750.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to inadequate chain/belt guard coverage.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes and contact an authorized Specialized retailer for a complimentary installation of an updated chain/belt guard.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for Specialized Vado and Como IGH e-bikes due to fall hazard; stop using and contact retailers for free repairs.

recalls.Description: BMC has announced a recall for all Kaius 01 bicycles and framesets due to a potential fall hazard. The affected models are available in various colors, including Peacock Spider, Carbon Black/Brushed Blue, Purple/White, White/Black, Turquoise/Black, Saffron/Black, and Stone Grey. The BMC brand is prominently displayed on the downtube, and only those units equipped with a V5 fork are included in this recall, necessitating a fork replacement.
recalls.Title: BMC Issues Recall for Kaius 01 Bicycles and Framesets Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BMC USA at 888-262-7755 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email kaius-fork-recall-USA@BMC-Switzerland.com. Additional information is available at www.bmc-switzerland.com/pages/platform/kaius-01-voluntary-recall or by visiting www.BMC-Switzerland.com and selecting "Learn More" under the "Voluntary Recall for Inspection" section.

Images.Caption: Recalled 2023 Kaius 01 ONE (White/Black)
Recalled 2023 Kaius 01 TWO (Turquoise/Black)
Recalled 2023 Kaius 01 THREE (Saffron/Black)
Recalled 2024 Kaius 01 ONE (Peacock Spider)
Recalled 2024 Kaius 01 TWO (Carbon Black/Brushed Blue)
Recalled 2024 Kaius 01 THREE (Purple/White)
Recalled 2024 Kaius 01 LTD (Peacock Spider)
Recalled 2026 Kaius 01 THREE (Stone Grey)
Recalled 2026 Kaius 01 Frameset (Stone Grey)

Injuries.Name: BMC has received two reports of the V5 fork steerers separating from the bicycle frame. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through authorized BMC bicycle dealers across the country and online from July 2022 to February 2025, priced between $4,500 and $12,000.

Importers.Name: BMC USA Corporation, of Scotts Valley, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fork steerer tube cracking or breaking, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled bicycles and contact an authorized BMC dealer for a complimentary inspection and fork replacement. BMC will directly reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: BMC recalls Kaius 01 bicycles and framesets due to a fall hazard. Stop using immediately and contact BMC for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Canyon Bicycles USA has announced a recall of all Speedmax CF triathlon bicycle models R073 and R41 equipped with V21 aerostems. These carbon fiber bikes are available in various color combinations, including black/red, black/blue, gray/orange, and red and black. The brand name 'Canyon' is located on the downtube, while 'Speedmax' appears on the top tube, and 'V21' is marked on the left side of the stem. The model number can be found on a sticker on the top of the seat tube, just beneath the saddle.
recalls.Title: Canyon Bicycles USA Issues Recall for Speedmax CF Triathlon Bikes Due to Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Canyon Bicycles USA at 833-226-9661 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. They can also visit www.canyon.com/en-us/contact/ for chat support or access further details at https://www.canyon.com/en-us/customer-service/repair-spares-warranty/speedmax-cf.html. Additionally, consumers can click on 'Speedmax Recall' at the bottom of the Canyon homepage for more information.

Products.Name: Canyon Speedmax CF Triathlon Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,400 (In addition, about 380 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Canyon Speedmax CF bicycle with V21 aerostem
The model number can be found on a sticker at the top of the seat tube, just below the saddle

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Canyon.com from May 2017 through July 2024, priced between $4,000 and $5,000.

Importers.Name: Canyon Bicycles USA Inc., of Carlsbad, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential cracking or breaking of the aerostem fork steerer tube.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the affected bicycles immediately and contact a Canyon Authorized Service Partner for a complimentary repair. The repair process will include the removal and inspection of the fork steerer tube, a replacement of the fork if necessary, and reinforcement of the clamping area of the fork steerer tube. Canyon will also reach out directly to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Canyon Bicycles recalls Speedmax CF triathlon bikes due to fall hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Super73 is recalling its 2024 Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core electric bicycles due to potential safety hazards. The Z Miami SE models are available in Bandit Black, Palladium Gray, and Astro Orange, while the Z Adventure Core is offered in Sandstorm. The model names and colors can be found on the purchase receipts. This recall affects specific models: Z Miami SE (900-00288, 900-00309, 900-00313) and Z Adventure Core (900-00308).
recalls.Title: Super73 Recalls Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core Electric Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Super73 at 888-841-3584 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email safety@super73.com. More details can be found at https://super73.com/pages/product-recall or by visiting https://super73.com and clicking on the 'Product Safety Advisory' link at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Super73 Z Miami SE e-bike - Model Year 2024
Recalled Super73 ZX Adventure Core e-bike - Model Year 2024
Disc brake caliper on the recalled e-bikes

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 21 reports of loose retaining pins and/or related brake failure, resulting in one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at Super73's Irvine, California store, various bicycle retailers nationwide, and online at https://Super73.com from April 2024 through September 2024, priced between $2,300 and $2,500.

Hazards.Name: Risk of brake failure due to a dislodged retaining pin in the disc brake calipers, leading to potential crashes and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes immediately and reach out to Super73 for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a new retaining pin and reimbursement of up to $50 for professional installation. Receipts for installation must be submitted for reimbursement. Super73 and its retailers will directly notify all affected purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Super73 recalls Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core e-bikes due to brake failure risks. Contact for free repair kit and installation reimbursement.

recalls.Description: Trek has issued a recall for its Electra Navigator Go! (2021 and 2022 models) and Electra Ace of Spades Go! e-bikes (2018, 2021, and 2022 models). The Navigator Go! features a Matte Nautilus Blue finish adorned with map and ocean graphics, while the Ace of Spades Go! is finished in Matte Black with a striking gold spade design. Both models prominently display the 'Electra' name on their respective chain guards and top tubes.
recalls.Title: Trek Recalls Electra Navigator and Ace of Spades E-Bikes Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ or by visiting www.trekbikes.com and selecting 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Electra Navigator Go! (2021 & 2022)
Recalled Electra Ace of Spades Go! (2021 & 2022) and Recalled Electra Ace of Spades Go! (2018)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the rear fender coming loose and making contact with the rear wheel, including one injury that involved a fractured shinbone, meniscus tear and scrapes.

Retailers.Name: Available at Trek-owned and independent bicycle shops nationwide, as well as online at www.electra.trekbikes.com from June 2018 through August 2023, priced around $3,000.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of rear fender detachment leading to potential loss of control and crash

Remedies.Name: Free repair to replace rear fender bolts available through authorized retailers

recalls.metaDescription: Trek recalls Electra Navigator and Ace of Spades e-bikes due to crash hazards. Contact Trek for a free repair.

recalls.Description: woom Bikes USA has announced a recall of their ORIGINAL 6 bicycles from model years 2016 to 2020, as well as the OFF 5, OFF AIR 5, OFF 6, and OFF AIR 6 bicycles from model years 2019 to 2020. These bikes are intended for children aged 10 to 14 and feature the brand name "woom" prominently displayed on the downtube and headtube. The ORIGINAL 6 models come in a variety of colors including red, green, blue, purple, and yellow, while the OFF mountain bikes are characterized by black frames with silver forks.
recalls.Title: woom Bikes USA Issues Recall for Children's OFF, OFF AIR, and ORIGINAL 6 Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled bicycles and contact woom Bikes USA at 855-966-6872 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at productsafetyUS@woom.com or visit https://faq.us.woombikes.com/category/88-recall-notice for more information. Additionally, consumers can register for the recall using the online crank checker tool available on www.woom.com.

Images.Caption: The recalled cranks have no space between the well with the woom logo and the pedal thread
Recalled OFF 5 – Black with silver carbon front fork, 24-inch wheels
Recalled OFF 6 – Black with silver carbon front fork, 26-inch wheels
Recalled OFF AIR 5 – Black with silver suspension front fork, 24-inch wheels
Recalled OFF AIR 6 – Black with silver suspension front fork, 26-inch wheels
Recalled ORIGINAL 6, 26-inch wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the crank breaking, including one report of an injury to a child.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.woom.com in the U.S. from September 2016 to October 2020, priced between $500 and $900.

Importers.Name: woom Bike USA LLC, of Austin, Texas

Hazards.Name: Crank breakage leading to loss of control, posing fall and crash risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the bicycles and contact woom Bikes USA for a free replacement crankset. An online crank checker tool is available at woom.com to verify if their bicycle is affected. Affected cranksets will be replaced at no cost, and installation should be performed by a professional bike repair technician. Consumers can submit their repair receipts for reimbursement of crank replacement costs. woom is directly contacting all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: woom Bikes USA recalls children's bicycles due to safety hazards. Stop using and contact woom for a free replacement crankset.

recalls.Description: Brompton Bicycle Inc. has issued a recall for their T-Line Foldable Bicycles due to a potential fall hazard. The bicycles measure 25.4 inches in height, 23.6 inches in width, and 12.6 inches in depth when folded. Each bike features the name 'Brompton' on the center tube of the frame and has a label on the back of the vertical seat tube that includes the frame number and serial number, along with a unique 'T' logo that differentiates this model from others. The bikes were available in a Blasted Titanium color.
recalls.Title: Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycles Recalled Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Brompton Bicycle Inc. at 833-507-7975 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@brompton.co.uk, or visit us.brompton.com/legal/t-line-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,080 (In addition, about 31 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycle
Recalled Brompton T-Line Foldable Bike in Folded Position
T-Line Logo on Label of Recalled T-Line Foldable Bike

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of incidents of steering misalignment or loss of steering control worldwide. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bike dealers, Brompton Junction Stores nationwide, and online at us.brompton.com from February 2024 through June 2024, priced between $5,225 and $5,500.

Importers.Name: Brompton Bicycle Inc., of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of losing control of steering due to misalignment between handlebar and front wheel

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles and contact an authorized Brompton dealer for a free repair to secure the steerer clamp bolts

recalls.metaDescription: Brompton recalls T-Line Foldable Bicycles due to a fall hazard. Contact Brompton for repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: REI is recalling its Co-op Cycles REV bicycles for children, available in 12-, 16-, and 20-inch sizes, from model years 2022 to 2024. The affected bicycles have serial numbers starting with 'ET22,' 'ET23,' '01VX,' or '07VX,' which can be found printed on the bottom bracket or down tube of the bike frame. The recall includes various color options for each bike size.
recalls.Title: REI Recalls Co-op Cycles REV Children's Bicycles Due to Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to REI at 800-426-4840 from 6 a.m. to 8 p.m. PT on weekdays or from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. Additional assistance is available through email or chat at rei.com/help, or visit https://www.rei.com/help?a=REI-Co-op-Cycles-REV-Bikes---id--He33EfUtSkKpMKyK7ZX27A for more details on the recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Co-op Cycles REV 12 Kids Bike (color: Pencil)
Recalled Co-op Cycles REV 16 Kids Bike with training wheels (color: Catch Up)
Recalled Co-op Cycles REV 20 Kids Bike with training wheels (color: Plum Burst)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the training wheels falling off. Injuries included reports of scrapes, bruising, and one involving broken arms.

Manufacturers.Name: RECREATIONAL EQUIPMENT INC. (REI), of Sumner, Washington

Retailers.Name: REI stores across the country and online at REI.com from March 2022 to May 2024, priced between $200 and $300.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to loose training wheel assembly

Remedies.Name: Free repair at local REI store or a repair kit shipped to consumers

recalls.metaDescription: REI recalls Co-op Cycles REV children's bicycles due to fall hazards. Learn how to get a free repair or kit.

recalls.Description: Babboe B.V. has issued a recall for all models of their two-wheeled and three-wheeled cargo bicycles, both electric and non-electric. These bicycles are designed for transporting children, pets, and light goods in urban settings. The recall is due to a potential safety issue that could pose a risk to users.
recalls.Title: Babboe Cargo Bicycles Recalled Due to Fall Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Babboe at their toll-free number 833-220-7867, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at service.us@babboe.com. More details can also be found on their website at www.internationalcargobike.com.

Products.Name: Babboe Cargo Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 300 (In addition, about 445 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Babboe Cargo Bicycle
Recalled Babboe Cargo Bicycle

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Various bicycle retailers across the U.S., including 718 Cyclery in New York City, A Street Bike Named Desire in Palo Alto, Going Dutch Bikes in Old Greenwich, Aika Trading LLC in Pacific Palisades, Bicycle Space in Washington, D.C., Clever Cycles in Portland, Dockside Boat in Princess Anne, Human Electric Hybrids LLC in Ypsilanti, La Dolce Velo in San Jose, PressWell in Hinsdale, and the Bike Center in Santa Monica, sold these bicycles from January 2011 through March 2024, priced between $3,500 and $7,000.

Hazards.Name: Risk of frame cracking and bending, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled bicycles and contact Babboe for a full refund and bicycle pickup.

recalls.metaDescription: Babboe recalls cargo bicycles due to fall hazards. Contact Babboe for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Retrospec is recalling Beaumont Plus ST City Bikes featuring disc brakes due to potential safety hazards. These bicycles are available in three colors: bluebell (blue), eggshell (white), and mint (green). The brand name 'Retrospec' appears on the bike frame, and the company's 'R' logo is located on the front fork. A silver label near the pedals indicates 'Retrospec, Made in Tianjin, China' along with a batch date of 12-15-2023.
recalls.Title: Retrospec Recalls Beaumont Plus ST City Bikes Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Retrospec at 888-488-2369 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recall@retrospec.com, or visit https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-beaumont-plus-w-disc-brakes-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Beaumont Plus ST with Disc Brakes in Bluebird
Recalled Beaumont Plus ST with Disc Brakes in Eggshell
Recalled Beaumont Plus ST with Disc Brakes in Mint
Recalled Beaumont Plus ST Bike label on bike frame

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle stores across the country from February 2024 to March 2024 for approximately $400.

Importers.Name: Xander Bicycle Corp., dba Retrospec, of Perris, California

Hazards.Name: Misalignment of front disc brake rotor and brake pads leading to brake failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles immediately and return them to the authorized dealer for free installation of a new front fork.

recalls.metaDescription: Retrospec recalls Beaumont Plus ST City Bikes due to brake failure risks. Learn how to get your bike repaired for free.

recalls.Description: Full Speed Ahead is recalling its Gossamer Pro AGX+ bicycle cranksets, model CK-6037, due to potential safety hazards. These cranksets were sold individually and as part of bicycles from brands like Cannondale, Canyon, Marin, Haro, Fuji, and Pinarello. The cranksets are identifiable by the engraving "FSA Gossamer Pro" on the front of the crankarm, with model and serial numbers located on the back next to the pedal threads. Only serial numbers starting with B1, B2, or B3 are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Full Speed Ahead Recalls Gossamer Pro AGX+ Cranksets Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected bicycles immediately and contact Full Speed Ahead at 877-743-3372 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be obtained via email at Recall-USA@fullspeedahead.com or by visiting www.fullspeedahead.com/en/support/recall. For updates, check the "Gossamer Pro AGX+ crankset recall" section on their website.

Products.Name: Gossamer Pro AGX+ Crankset
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,300 (In addition, about 250 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled FSA Gossamer Pro AGX+ Bicycle Crankset
Recalled FSA Gossamer Pro AGX+ Bicycle Crankset

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 277 reports of bicycle crankarms loosening or detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers nationwide and online through brands such as Cannondale, Canyon, Marin, Haro, Fuji, and Pinarello from January 2022 to June 2023, priced around $190 for the crankset and between $1,700 and $4,000 for complete bicycles.

Importers.Name: Full Speed Ahead Inc., of Mukilteo, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of crank arms loosening or detaching, leading to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles with the recalled cranksets and contact Full Speed Ahead for free dealer installation of a replacement crankset.

recalls.metaDescription: Full Speed Ahead recalls Gossamer Pro AGX+ cranksets due to fall risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Delta Cycle and Dimension have issued a recall for their Stem Raisers, specifically model numbers TD3318B, TD3318S, TD3418B, TD3418S for Delta Cycle, and SM1977 and SM1979 for Dimension. These products, available in black and silver, range from five to seven inches in height. The recall is prompted by the potential for the stem raisers to shift during use, which can unexpectedly alter the position of the bicycle's handlebars, leading to a risk of falls.
recalls.Title: Delta Cycle Recalls Bicycle Stem Raisers Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Delta Cycle at 800-474-6615 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email stemrecall@designbydelta.com or visit www.designbydelta.com/stemrecall for more details.

Products.Name: Delta Cycle Stem Raisers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 500,000 (In addition, about 8,510 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Delta Cycle and Dimension Stem Raiser (TD3418B or SM1979)
Recalled Delta Cycle and Dimension Stem Raiser (TD3418S)
Recalled Delta Cycle and Dimension Stem Raiser (TD3418B or SM1977)
Recalled Delta Cycle and Dimension Stem Raiser (TD3318S)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a stem raiser shifting during use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, local bicycle shops nationwide, and online at Designbydelta.com and Amazon.com from January 1998 through January 2024 for approximately $46.

Importers.Name: Delta Cycle, of Foxborough, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to unexpected handlebar shift

Remedies.Name: Stop using the stem raisers and contact Delta Cycle for a free repair

recalls.metaDescription: Delta Cycle recalls Stem Raisers due to fall risk; contact them for a free repair. Stay safe while cycling!

recalls.Description: GT is recalling its Model Year 2019 to 2023 LaBomba bicycles, including the 24" and 26" models, LaBomba Pro, LaBomba Rigid, and LaBomba 26" framesets. These bicycles were available in various colors such as sea green, green, dusty blue, blue, purple, and red. The model name 'LaBomba' is displayed on the top-tube of the frame, while 'GT' is located on the downtube.
recalls.Title: GT Recalls LaBomba Bicycles Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact GT at 800-843-2453 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at ridersupport@gtbicycles.com or visit www.gtbicycles.com/pages/recalls-safety for more details.

Products.Name: GT LaBomba 24" Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,040 (In addition, about 324 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled GT LaBomba 24"
Recalled GT LaBomba Rigid
Recalled GT LaBomba Pro

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of damage to the bicycle headtube/downtube weld, causing the bicycle headtube to separate from the bicycle frame. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers across the country from October 2018 to September 2023, priced between $400 and $1,450.

Importers.Name: GT Bicycles LLC, of Aliso Viejo, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential damage at the headtube/downtube weld.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles immediately and contact an authorized GT dealer for a complimentary replacement frame.

recalls.metaDescription: GT recalls LaBomba bicycles from 2019 to 2023 due to fall hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Cannondale is recalling its 2021 to 2023 Model Year 26" Dave bicycles and framesets, available in deep teal and stealth gray. These bicycles feature the name "Dave" on the top-tube and "Cannondale" on the downtube. The recall is prompted by concerns regarding the headtube/downtube weld, which may become compromised and detach from the frame, leading to potential falls and injuries.
recalls.Title: Cannondale Issues Recall for Dave Bicycles Due to Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cannondale at 800-245-3872, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or via email at ridersupport@cannondale.com. More information is available at www.cannondale.com/en/safety-and-recalls or by visiting www.cannondale.com and selecting "Recalls & Notices" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: 2021 to 2023 Cannondale Dave Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 660 (In addition, about 113 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Cannondale 26" Dave bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of damage to the bicycle headtube/downtube weld, causing the bicycle headtube to separate from the bicycle frame. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle stores nationwide and on Cannondale.com from June 2021 to September 2023, priced around $1,300.

Importers.Name: Cycling Sports Group Inc., dba Cannondale, of Wilton, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Risk of falls and injuries due to potential headtube/downtube weld failure.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles and contact an authorized Cannondale dealer for a free frame replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Cannondale recalls Dave bicycles from 2021 to 2023 due to serious fall hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement frame.

recalls.Description: Box Components is recalling its BMX Race Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta bicycle stems, which are designed to secure the fork steerer and handlebars. These stems feature dual-tone gray stripes that are laser-etched at an angle on the sides, along with the 'Boxone' name and logo. They were sold individually and came in various colors such as black, red, blue, gold, purple, and silver. The bolts used in these stems measure 6 mm in diameter and 20 mm in length.
recalls.Title: Box Components Recalls BMX Race Bicycle Stems Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Box Components at 800-797-6880 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Thursday. You can also email orders@boxcomponents.com or visit https://boxcomponents.com/pages/recall-claim-form-delta-stem to file a recall claim. For additional details, check https://boxcomponents.com/pages/recall-bulletin or go to https://boxcomponents.com and select 'Recalls and Bulletins' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: BMX Race Box One Oversized Bicycle Stems

Images.Caption: Recalled Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta Stem

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a stem faceplate's cracking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through BMX Race dealers across the country and online at https://boxcomponents.com from January 2023 to October 2023, priced around $100.

Distributors.Name: The Cycle Group, Dba Box Components, from Anaheim, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of handlebars loosening due to cracking faceplate, leading to potential loss of control and injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the affected stems and contact Box Components for a full refund or a complimentary replacement, including shipping.

recalls.metaDescription: Box Components recalls BMX Race bicycle stems due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Flat River Group is recalling HolaBelle NextGen children's bicycles in sizes 10-inch, 12-inch, 16-inch, and 20-inch. Available in colors such as black, blue, red, purple, and pink, these bicycles feature the NextGen logo prominently displayed on the frame. The recall is due to safety concerns that could lead to crashes and injuries among young riders. The affected models do not comply with U.S. safety standards for bicycles.
recalls.Title: Recall of HolaBelle NextGen Children's Bicycles Due to Safety Risks and Federal Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Flat River Group at 833-857-1010 anytime, or visit www.Flatrivergroup.com/nextgen-recall/ for more details. Alternatively, visit www.Flatrivergroup.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled NextGen 10-inch Children's Bike (Black)
Recalled NextGen 12-inch Children's Bike (Blue)
Recalled NextGen 16-inch Children's Bike (Pink)
Recalled NextGen 20-inch Children's Bike (Red)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Army & Air Force Exchange Service, Amazon, Fingerhut, Groupon, HSN, Kohl's, Lowe's, Macy's, Overstock, QVC, Target, Tractor Supply, and Walmart between October 2020 and November 2022, priced between $80 and $135.

Importers.Name: Flat River Group, of Belding, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with U.S. safety standards leading to potential crash and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the bicycles, keep them away from children, and contact the company for a full refund process.

recalls.metaDescription: Flat River Group recalls HolaBelle NextGen children's bicycles due to safety hazards. Immediate action recommended for consumers.

recalls.Description: SRAM has announced a recall for all 12-speed shift brake levers sold as standalone upgrade or replacement kits. These brake levers feature black hoods and may pose a risk to riders. The clamp bolt on the brake lever can loosen, which could lead to a crash while riding, as the brake lever is essential for controlling the bicycle's braking system.
recalls.Title: SRAM 12-Speed Shift Brake Levers Recalled Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SRAM at 800-346-2928, available from 11 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found at https://www.sram.com/en/service/recalls by selecting 'Recall Notice,' or by visiting www.sram.com and navigating to 'Recalls' under 'Service/Support.'

Products.Name: 12-Speed Shift Brake Levers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 61,300 (In addition, about 2,940 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled SRAM Apex AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Rival eTap AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Red eTap AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Force eTap AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Force eTap AXS shift brake lever

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at NordicTrack.com, Amazon.com, and DicksSportingGoods.com from January 2022 to November 2023, priced between $500 and $600.

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash due to loose clamp bolt on brake lever

Remedies.Name: Free brake installation inspection and tightening or replacement of clamp bolt assembly if necessary

recalls.metaDescription: SRAM recalls 12-speed shift brake levers due to crash risk. Inspect your levers and contact SRAM for a free inspection.

recalls.Description: Pacific Cycle is recalling its Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret Electric Bikes due to a potential fire hazard. The Cabrillo models, identified by model numbers R7583BPS and R7585BPS, feature a rear storage rack and were available in sand or gray colors. The Minaret model, with the number R7586BPS, is offered in black with red and gray accents or in a step-through frame style. Both models have 'Ascend' printed on the downtube and their respective names on the chain stay, along with a service label located on the seat tube above the bottom bracket.
recalls.Title: Pacific Cycle Recalls Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret E-Bikes Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Pacific Cycle at 877-564-2261 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at customerservice@pacificycle.com. More information is available online at www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls or by visiting www.pacific-cycle.com and clicking on 'Safety Notices & Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Ascend Cabrillo Electric Bike (sand)
Recalled Ascend Cabrillo Electric Bike (gray)
Recalled Ascend Minaret Electric Bike (black)
Recalled Ascend Cabrillo Electric Bike (serial number location)
Recalled Ascend Minaret or Cabrillo Electric Bike service label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the battery catching on fire, resulting in one injury of second-degree burns.

Retailers.Name: Bass Pro Shops nationwide

Importers.Name: Pacific Cycle Inc., of Madison, Wisconsin

Distributors.Name: Bass Pro LLC, of Springfield, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Improperly assembled wiring harness posing a fire risk during charging

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the recalled electric bikes; confirm recall status online and return for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Pacific Cycle recalls Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret E-Bikes due to fire hazards. Check your bike's model and return for a refund.

recalls.Description: Aventon has announced a recall of its Class 2 Sinch.2 electric bicycles due to potential safety hazards. These folding e-bikes, available in silver (quicksilver) and blue (sapphire), feature front and rear tires along with foldable handlebars. The brand name 'Aventon' is located on the downtube, while 'Sinch.2' is printed on the chain stay connecting the rear wheel to the frame.
recalls.Title: Aventon Recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bikes Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ride Aventon at 866-300-3311 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday. Alternatively, contact them via email at info@aventon.com or visit their website at https://www.aventon.com/pages/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Quicksilver)
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Sapphire)
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Sapphire) with handlebars folded
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike - portable

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the recalled e-bikes accelerating unexpectedly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers nationwide and online at BestBuy.com and Eriksbikeshop.com from April 2023 to August 2023, priced around $1,800.

Importers.Name: Ride Aventon Inc., of Brea, California

Hazards.Name: Unexpected acceleration leading to loss of control and potential crash and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled e-bikes and contact an authorized Ride Aventon dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes a software update to the controller.

recalls.metaDescription: Aventon recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bikes due to crash risks. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair.
